prior_criticals_resolved.task_1_return_type_missing: FIXED — Task 1 step 4 and acceptance criteria explicitly require returning (thread, master)
prior_criticals_resolved.task_5_master_state_flows_hallucination: FIXED — Task 5 line 116 explicitly states 'There is no valid master.state.flows API on mitmproxy.master.Master — do not assert on flow counts from the master object'
prior_criticals_resolved.task_5_wrong_dependency_claim_for_flowcl: FIXED — Task 5 correctly attributes FlowCleanup to Task 2; no mention of Task 4 for FlowCleanup

findings.issue: Task 3 Details section line 132 says '_start_mitmproxy currently returns only thread. After this task it should return (thread, master)'. This is stale and contradictory: Task 1 (which Task 3 depends on) was updated in iteration 1 to own this return type change — Task 1 step 4 explicitly states 'Return (thread, master) from _start_mitmproxy()'. An implementer executing Task 3 will find _start_mitmproxy() already returning (thread, master) from Task 1, but the hint says it 'currently returns only thread'. This misleads the implementer into thinking the return type change is still pending work for Task 3.
findings.fix: Update Task 3 Details line 132 to: '_start_mitmproxy() already returns (thread, master) after Task 1. The master reference is available to _restart_mitmproxy() via the module-level or closure pattern described in the Master and addon reference access section above.'

findings.issue: Task 2 TDD anchor tests in test/test_proxy_addon.py include test_flowcleanup_response_clears_content which must assert flow.request.content is cleared. The existing FakeRequest class in test/test_proxy_addon.py (inside the make_flow fixture) only defines host, url, and headers attributes — it has no content attribute. The test writer must add content to FakeRequest, but the task gives no guidance on this extension.
findings.fix: Add to Task 2 TDD anchor or Details section: 'The FakeRequest helper in test/test_proxy_addon.py needs a content attribute for FlowCleanup tests. Extend it with self.content = b"" or pass content as a constructor argument when creating FakeFlow for FlowCleanup tests.'

findings.issue: Task 2 edge case note for websocket_end says 'check mitmproxy API for correct approach (del flow vs flow.kill() vs content clearing)'. This remains vague and could mislead an implementer into using flow.kill() which sends a TCP RST — inappropriate after the connection has ended. This issue was noted in iteration 1 as minor and was not fixed.
findings.fix: Clarify: 'In websocket_end(), clearing flow.websocket.messages and flow.request.content is sufficient. Do NOT call flow.kill() — the connection is already terminated at this point and kill() would be a no-op at best, disruptive at worst.'
